reasoning: Alina Habba is currently the acting U.S. Attorney for the District of New Jersey as of late November 2025. Her appointment was ruled unlawful by a district court in August 2025 in a ruling that was stayed pending appeal to the Third Circuit. The Third Circuit heard oral arguments on October 20, 2025, but no ruling has been issued as of November 23. There are only about 38 days until the December 31, 2025 resolution date. Even if the Third Circuit rules against her before December 31, the mandate typically does not issue for 21 days, and the government would very likely seek a rehearing en banc or a stay pending an appeal to the Supreme Court, which would stall any removal well past the end of the year. Additionally, it is practically impossible for the Senate to receive a new nominee and confirm them before the winter recess in December. Given the Trump administration's demonstrated commitment to keeping her in the position through procedural maneuvers (such as withdrawing her nomination so she could be acting, and firing her court-appointed successor), I confidently estimate a very high probability that she will still hold the office on December 31, 2025.
